elements.description: PROPELLER FAN TYPE (400 CFM.), mounted at the top wall of ซักล้าง with a dashed leader line to an exterior louvre
CEILING FAN (120 CFM.), inside the ground-floor ห้องน้ำ, paired with a 6"x6" exhaust grille
EXHAUST AIR GRILL W/INS, ขนาด 6"x6", paired 1-to-1 with the ground-floor bathroom's 120 CFM ceiling fan

warnings: This ground-floor mechanical sheet has NO air-conditioning units at all -- only a laundry propeller fan and a bathroom ceiling-fan/exhaust-grille pair. This is a genuine and notable difference from house #2's single M-01 sheet, which combined AC (for bedrooms) and ventilation fans on one sheet for a 1-story house. Here, because this is a 2-story house, all 3 bedrooms and their AC units are on the upper floor (M-02, page 63) -- the ground floor only has common/wet-area rooms (dining, kitchen, laundry, carport, storage, living/relax) that don't call for AC per this design.

GRID: a house-wide grid master (บ้าน_เล็ก_2ชั้น_01_หน้า00_gridline.json) appeared partway through this extraction batch; grid columns/rows read from the same printed dimension chain as the electrical EE-03..EE-06 sheets (1/2/3 x, E/D/C/B/A y plus dummy 1'/3') match the master exactly.

A small numbered staircase-tread sequence ('7 6 5 4 3 2 1') is visible inside the stairwell glyph -- this is a standard architectural stair-tread count annotation, not a mechanical equipment item, and is excluded from the elements list.

This is the first of 2 mechanical sheets in the drawing set (M-01 of 2, per title block drawing no. 'M-58110') -- unlike house #2's single M-01-of-1 sheet, this house splits AC/ventilation across 2 sheets by floor, matching the same 2-floor split pattern already seen on the electrical EE-03/EE-04 and EE-05/EE-06 sheet pairs.
